arguments.name: explore-aot-issues
arguments.prompt:
Explore the .NET project in /tmp/sv-0211bc9be9454601bcecb76d832ace84/before/ to identify all AOT (Ahead-of-Time compilation) compatibility issues. This is an Azure SDK project (Azure.ResourceManager).

Focus on:

1. The .csproj file - check if `IsAotCompatible` is set, trimming properties, etc.
2. Any use of reflection-based JSON serialization (System.Text.Json without source generators)
3. Any `JsonConverter` implementations that use reflection
4. Any use of `MakeGenericType`, `MakeGenericMethod`, or other reflection APIs
5. Any `Activator.CreateInstance` calls
6. Any `Type.GetType` or assembly scanning
7. Check the `Shared/` directory for AOT-unfriendly patterns
8. Check model serialization files (*.Serialization.cs) for patterns
9. Check for `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` or `[RequiresUnreferencedCode]` attributes that may be needed
10. Look at the `ArmClient.cs`, `ArmClientOptions.cs`, `ArmResource.cs` files for reflection usage
11. Check `GenericOperationSource.cs` carefully - generic operation sources often use reflection
12. Check `ManagedServiceIdentityTypeV3Converter.cs` - custom converters often need AOT updates
13. Check `NextLinkOperationImplementation.cs` - operation implementations may use reflection
14. Check all files under `Resources/Generated/Models/` and `Common/Generated/Models/` and `ManagementGroup/Generated/Models/` for serialization patterns
15. Look for any `[UnconditionalSuppressMessage]` attributes that might already exist
16. Check `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` for any existing AOT-related attributes
17. Look at the `Directory.Build.props` and `Directory.Packages.props` for build configuration
18. Check `Extensions/ArmClientBuilderExtensions.cs` for DI registration patterns that might use reflection

List every file and specific line that needs changes, and describe what change is needed. Be thorough and list ALL issues found.
